source_urdf_description: Plain-text URDF expanded from xacro for the KR810 (a810) arm, provided as an external local asset outside this repository.
conversion_steps: Loaded assets/kr810.urdf directly with mujoco.MjModel.from_xml_path (MuJoCo built-in URDF importer).
Exported the compiled model to canonical MJCF with mujoco.mj_saveLastXML.
Set compiler meshdir to meshes/a810 and shortened per-mesh file attributes to basenames (relative path only, no absolute path).
Added ee_site to the end_effector body at local origin (pos 0 0 0, quat 1 0 0 0).
No actuators, no geometry resizing, no manual repositioning were added.

operational_limits_rad.note: Operational sampling limits for use in target sampling and DLS, not proven mechanical hard stops. For joint_1, joint_3, joint_5, joint_6, joint_7 the URDF encodes a full +/-2pi revolute range (a continuous-joint convention expressed as revolute); these are carried through unchanged as operational bounds per project plan. joint_2 and joint_4 use the URDF's asymmetric revolute limits directly, which are the only limits available for those joints.

end_effector_definition: KR810 end-effector link reference frame. This is NOT an officially calibrated TCP, tool tip, or flange calibration frame.
known_limitations: ee_site is a reference frame at the end_effector link origin only; no official TCP/tool-tip/flange calibration exists for this robot.

MuJoCo's built-in URDF-to-MJCF compilation fuses fixed (jointless) child bodies into their nearest jointed parent body. As a result the URDF fixed links base, link2, link4, link6 and the fixed joints dummy, jointPL2, jointPL4, jointPL6 do not appear as separate named bodies/joints in kr810.xml; their geometry and inertial properties are algebraically composed (rigid transform + mass/inertia combination) into the parent body. This does not change the 7-DOF kinematic chain, nq, nv, or joint order.

MJCF has no native per-joint velocity-limit attribute, so joint velocity limits are not embedded inside kr810.xml itself; they are recorded here and in configs/robot_config.json from the source URDF <limit velocity=...> values.

For joint_1, joint_3, joint_5, joint_6, joint_7 the URDF's +/-2pi revolute range is a continuous-joint convention, not a verified mechanical hard stop; it is used only as an operational sampling limit.

All URDF inertial blocks use an identical placeholder diagonal inertia tensor (0.003, 0.003, 0.003); these are not CAD-derived dynamics and are carried through unchanged since Stage 2 covers kinematic asset integration only, not dynamics validation.
No MuJoCo actuators are defined (nu=0); Tier 0-4 does not require actuation.
